item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First thought: this SCP could probably join SCP-085 for a time.

Second thought: yes, but it would traumatize the poor girl even further.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still like it, even though parts of it show their age. One point that does stand out as really not very good is the arrest log, though, that is superfluous and not very well written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cool concept. Imo, an acceptable amount of detail. The experiment logs seem a little lazy though. Most of them are just varying beautiful women. I really think the foundation would be smart enough to realize that this SCP has a lot more potential than just turning into women. But, unfortunately, I can't downvote an article I like nor would my downvote really matter in the grand scheme of things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-056: The Original Chad

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Apologies. I didn't mean to discourage him, I just felt this criticism would be better placed on some of the newer articles, and not one with a high rating.

But I understand, and I will refrain from this in the future.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Doombringer106, please don't discourage site members from commenting on SCP articles. If they want to respond to a piece, they're free to do so; it doesn't matter how old the article is. We don't stop allowing people to voice their opinions on works just because something is old.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's great that you are reading up on the series 1 skips….

But remember, these are series 1. This is literally from 2009. Put your feedback on some of the newer articles… Half of the authors aren't even around now. Nobody is gonna change this scp. Its history

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: im gonna be a broken record and say cool concept aged badly. given what this thing might become if exposed to retain SCP's or hell even just certain doctors and agents it really shouldn't have free reign of a facility. I don't think it needs upgraded to Keter as it can be easily contained but its not actually being contained

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, interesting concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting concept.

That's over two soda cans of tranquilizer in those agents' guns - and they'll need it, because with any lethal weapon, you'd think they'd kill SCP-056 out of envy. It's a good thing all they can do to it is stun it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a cool concept, but it's aged badly and the containment procedures are really stupid. Downvoting in hopes of an eventual rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to upvote because I like the concept, but have this nagging feeling that there is just something missing, though I can't quite put my finger on it at the moment. There are also parts that seem to cross the line from mere mind effecting to cognitohazard or memetic (depending on whether the effects persist after one is no longer in it's presence.).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoting because of great concept, could use some retooling however.

item.title: Re: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded: (ﾉ◕ヮ◕)ﾉ*:・ﾟ✧*:・ﾟεїз εїз εїз

item.title: Re: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
I think you mean "a US version of a word which reduces consonants of the original, I-was-here-first British version."

#justsayin'

item.title: Re: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Crazy! I've never seen a British variant of a word that doubled consonants of an American version before.

item.title: Re: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
According to the Oxford dictionary, 'tranquiliser' is spelt either this way or 'tranquillizer'. Personally, I use the former.

I'm actually from Canada and therefore use Canadian English, but I'm just used to writing/typing all those extra U's and 'S's instead of 'Z's; it's just easier to use UK English as default.

item.title: Re: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
the spelling 'error' might have been due to the spell check's language being British and not American

The British variant uses two l's?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you wanna show a rewrite to me, as rewrite team captain, I can look, but I'm not too inclined to give an article with this high of a rating a fresh rewrite.

item.title: Re: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Right. Sorry. Won't happen again.

(Also the spelling 'error' might have been due to the spell check's language being British and not American.)

item.title: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
I'll just fix some grammatical errors and some less obvious things (like referring to an SCP by its number)

enoch_exe_inc, your recent series of edits actually introduced errors into the piece (most noticeably, "tranquilizer" is the correct spelling) and also corrected some things that were alright grammatically (including the name "Kennith"). In regards to referring to the SCP by its number, given the age of the article, I'm inclined to keep that as a stylistic choice on behalf of the author.

As such, your edits have been reverted. Please obtain staff permission prior to making any further changes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article is in dire need of an update/rewrite. I'll probably write one on my sandbox, but I'll just fix some grammatical errors and some less obvious things (like referring to an SCP by its number).

EDIT: Nevermind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this thing would implode if exposed to Dr Gears.

This object's SCProcedures are a little odd, considering the Foundation's usual approach. I don't like many things that have 'freedom of the site'.

item.title: Re: Adendum
item.content:encoded:
I hope you don't mind the necro, but if this item is a "I do it better" thing, what if we expose the item to a person that wants to be better at being selfdestructive or better at being ugly. The knee-jerk response may be to say those people do not exist, but they do.

Also, if the guards try and sedate it, wouldn't it sedate the guards 'better'?

Or does this object just always do what is seen as' traditionally' better or better by it's own estimation.

item.title: Re: Re my previous edit
item.content:encoded: Discussion in staff chat agreed with the edit, also reinforced by Zyn's post above. Article reverted back to the fix.

item.title: Re my previous edit
item.content:encoded:
Sorry for editing without discussing it first and all (I have since felt the wrath/mild admonishment of TheDuckman), but I do think the removal of 'human' should stand.

It's a shape-shifting entity, whose 'natural' form it is impossible to view; it displays no apparent life signs, and theoretically could not exist if unobserved. It's not like you can even say that it only takes human forms either, so really, it seems kinda odd to assume it's a human being.

Just saying, would it be justified to reinforce this change?

item.title: Re: Dear Staff,
item.content:encoded: Hmmm. It seems okay to me, given the context. It's sort of like if the article states the object is "a fish with five heads" and then later refers to it as an ursine rather than a piscine. It would be a content change to swap "ursine" with "piscine", but a reasonable correction.

item.title: Dear Staff,
item.content:encoded:
In regards to the previous edit, may I have staff opinion if it is counted as a change in content (and thus, should be reverted for changing the article's intent)?

Removed human - if we can't see its natural form, and it has no normal life signs, how do we know it's human?

item.title: Re: Mirrors
item.content:encoded: Anytime someone asks "What would happen if…" the correct response is "Write that tale up and tell us!"

item.title: Mirrors
item.content:encoded: Ok 1 person in this whole discussion brought up this concept… but not fully (and since this post will likely be lost to the archives for eternity, unanswered) What would happen if 056 was left in a room walled entirely of mirrors with a single one-way mirror for observaion? I mean… that would be one way of bringing out a "true form" or creating a never-ending paradox of world-shattering proportions. Regardless of the fact that it won't happen, would be nice to see an experiment log or addendum including this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When questioned about other SCPs, it showed fear and occasionally hatred, and refused to speak about any of them, even objects classified as Safe.

Hmm… Maybe it cannot transform in other SCPs?

Anyways, a very interesting concept, and even with the weird procedures, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not downvoting myself, but yea - the whole article could use some serious updating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Voted to be best looking woman on facility." Downvoting hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This object has demonstrated malicious (if, admittedly, very petty) intent, and is clearly interested in breaking containment if presented with an opportunity to do so. Why are we allowing it to roam free across the site?

The premise of the article is great, but so long as those containment protocols remain in place, I can't justify anything but a downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That may not work out quite the way you'd hope. (Murphy is my Containment Officer.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know what we should do…

we should show it someone who's contained poorly.

"You think that's being contained?! I can be contained better than ANYONE!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1 for lack of secure containment in the procedure. I hate uncontained/uncontainable/site-wandering skips with the blazing fury of a thousand stars.

Great concept, would upvote except for containment issues.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be fair, it seems containment is fairly easy as long as no living being is in the room.
SCP-056 is either a chair or an expensive camera.

But i admit while the concept is interesting, (the embodiment of one-upping) the description just make it sound like the embodiment of douchebaggery, potentially dangerous but not as deranging as it could be.

I guess it could be made in a more mysterious and less self explanatory way, since the current version isn't of much interest, even though it still raises questions about the way it works. (Does it just mimic and adapt? Does it has innate knowledge of the thing it tries to "beat"?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same as several others here +1 for the SCP being awesome plus a -1 for the Containment Procedures being totally retarded equals no vote at this time. It makes me sad because I'd like to upvote it, but I can't upvote an SCP whose property is being better than anything you could ever throw at it, and whose containment protocol is, "eh, whatever, just make sure it doesn't wander too far."

item.title: Addendum 3
item.content:encoded: This addendum doesn't fit the SCP how I see it. It might for some, but it takes away a big part of the mystery of it's purpose or motivations. The whole article sets the SCP up as quite passive and I always interpreted it as a non-sentient manifestation of jealousy. A sort of apparition that merely parrots someone's qualities, but enhanced. With the addendum mine and other interpretations like mine get blown out of the water. It takes away the mystery and forces the reader to interpret it as a sentient being which is yet another 'thing that feeds on human X' in this case jealousy. Which makes it a lot less interesting, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Having now looked up "provenance" and expanded my vocabulary, I completely agree.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I saw that as well, and I'm entirely neutral on this; I just wanted to point it out and have it looked at. Provenance is serious business, after all.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Although I'm inclined to believe that there's a very good chance you are indeed the author of this article, I have - for now - reverted your edits. This will be discussed by staff and if people who are in a better position to judge whether you are the author decide that this is all kosher, we can always revert to your latest edit.

Until that time, please do not revert back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Normally, I'd jump on the "Prove it!" bandwagon with both feet and guns blazing - except I looked at the age of his account. Then I had to look at the system member list. Twice.

#15 to join the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only confirmation I could provide is that I wrote "The Black Cat" article on the original site, and 'The Bringer of Conflict' (the updated version is way better than the original.) SCP-056 needed an update.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As much as this page needs a cleanup, has staff confirmed that CR056 is in fact the original author of this article? The only provenance we have is that Lt Masipag copied this article over from EditThis.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Historical article and been around for a very long time…

…but I feel it could be brought up to date a bit, mostly for the reasons above. It's an SCP that (to a level) trolls people around it and it's:

…kept in a room of its choosing, with whatever furnishings it expresses desire for. Level 1 personnel and above may interact with SCP-056 at any time they choose, for a time length not exceeding two hours.

That's a bit much for a Euclid item.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Part of my wants to praise this as some sort of SCP that is so good at being better than others that it has most anyone in contact with it trying to win its favor or something, but part of me feels like this is still just a SCP that gets way too much of what it wants.

More so because it seems to piss off half the people it meets. Also, internet connection? When would that EVER be a thing the Foundation would allow? Am I Right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-056 is to be kept in a room of its choosing, with whatever furnishings it expresses desire for.

The subject is to be allowed access to any object it desires

It may wander in Research Sector ██ as it wills

I am downvoting for multiple examples of nonsensical containment, just a few of which has been quoted. I do not agree with allowing this humanoid to live so lavishly and luxuriously. I feel this kind of idea has been better done with The Gayest Man Alive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens with 056 is left alone in a room with a mirror? I'm thinking it will either just turn into a more polished and ornate mirror, or cause some sort of loop and [DATA EXPUNGED]

item.title: Re: Use of 056 to Terminate 682
item.content:encoded: It becomes something that's better at hating all life than 682

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
See, THIS is clever. On the surface, it's a Mary Sue of the highest order. But that's the point. That's what it wants you to think. It wants — or needs — jealousy and sycophantic praise. It's not A Mary Sue. It's THE Mary Sue. That's how it lives. That's its purpose in life, to make you fell inadequate. That's all it ever does. Bravo for taking the very essence of Mary Sue and making it into a convincing piece of writing.

EDIT: Now that I've reread the containment procedures, what the flying floop. The article is fine, but the containment procedures are all kinds of messed up. Bad tone, give it whatever, let it walk around wherever, etc. You'd think the research staff would do everything they could to screw this thing over, given how much they hate it. At the very least, they would give it only the bare minimum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent, but needs a re-write. I'm not 056-ish enough to consider myself a candidate for authorship, however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the concept of this, but not the execution.

Neutral for now, I suppose. I want to downvote it, but I enjoy the idea enough to refrain.

item.title: Re: Use of 056 to Terminate 682
item.content:encoded:
Do not try to kill the 682.

Edit: Darnit, I could swear I got that Matrix quote right.

item.title: Use of 056 to Terminate 682
item.content:encoded: Sooooo, I'm just wondering, what would happen if 056 were introduced to 682? 056 is always capable of one uping anyone, and since it killed that Delta that tried to attack it, and 682 hates all life, 056 would then some how one up 682 and kill it right? I may be wrong, but I am a complete noob so, sorry if i am.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like a lot of this article, but there are plenty of bits that are hard to swallow. The containment procedures, recovery log, and final note are all a bit icky. Downvoting for now, but an update could easily change that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like this one very much. Just sits on a kinda okay concept without going anywhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted for "give it whatever it wants" containment procedures. The SCiP itself is okay. Perhaps a little sardonic. But no, no we will not give it whatever it wants because this isn't a resort.
